cot_prefix: Okay, let's read through the passage to find the answer to the question about the American scientists' search group. First, the question is asking what the search group formed by some American scientists did. The answer choices are A through D, and I need to pick the correct one. In the passage, the relevant section mentions that in 1975, some American scientists formed a search group. They used an underwater camera that took pictures every seventy seconds. Some of the pictures seemed to show a red-brown creature with a four-meter-long body and a very ugly head on the end of a four-meter neck. This made many people begin to believe in the monster, but even today, we can't be certain. Looking at the answer choices: - (A) found the monster itself: The passage doesn't say they found the monster, only that pictures seemed to show a creature. - (B) found a huge cave under water: This was mentioned in the previous part of the text when talking about underwater TV cameras being used, but not by the American scientists. - (C) believed that there wasn't any monster at all: The passage doesn't say they believed there wasn't a monster. - (D) took some pictures which seemed to show a monster:
